blocks.text:
# Stop Work Authority Process

Rig Class(s): All Land Rigs

## 1.0. Purpose

The purpose of this process is to provide an outline of the Stop Work Authority Process (SWA) for employees, contractors, and visitors.

## 2.0. Introduction

2.1. Each employee has the authority and responsibility any time they feel an unsafe condition exists, an unsafe work practice is occurring, or improper instructions are given by a co-worker, H&P supervisor, 3rd party Representative, or Operator Representative that may cause harm to people, impact on the environment, or property damage.

- **Everyone** have the right and duty to refuse to perform any task that they do not completely understand or may feel is unsafe.
- **Everyone** have the authority to stop all work being performed by other employees if they perceive the work is unsafe.
blocks.source_heading_path: Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority
blocks.additive_detail: KCAD explicitly makes refusal of a task not fully understood a duty and authorizes stopping work being performed by other employees if perceived unsafe; those specific trigger/authority details are not stated in this HP policy chunk.
blocks.ai_reasoning: HP candidate 0 is the closest match because it assigns each employee authority and responsibility to intervene when unsafe conditions, practices, or instructions are perceived, which aligns with KCAD's stop-work authority policy. The match is a Variant rather than Equivalent because KCAD is Kuwait-scoped and explicitly adds refusal of work not fully understood plus authority to stop other employees' work.

blocks.text:
# Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority

KCA Deutag is committed to achieving safe, effective, trouble-free operations. We firmly believe that an incident and accident free environment is possible 365 days a year and every incident is preventable.

Health and Safety of employees is one of the Core Values of KCA DEUTAG. Our employees and contractors have the right to a safe job at a safe and secure worksite.
blocks.ai_reasoning: The KCAD text is a high-level policy/value statement about safe work and worker safety rights, while HP candidate 0 is an operational authority statement telling employees when they may and must intervene. They align on the stop-work safety domain, but differ in function and practical effect, so the best fit is Complementary rather than Variant or Equivalent.

blocks.edited_text:
# Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority

KCA Deutag is committed to achieving safe, effective, trouble-free operations. We firmly believe that an incident and accident free environment is possible 365 days a year and every incident is preventable.

blocks.text: Our employees and contractors have the right to a safe job at a safe and secure worksite. Our vision is that everyone has a responsibility and obligation to stop any work, which is not performed in a safe way.
blocks.additive_detail: KCAD explicitly extends stop-work right/obligation to contractors in addition to employees and states a right to a safe and secure worksite; the HP chunk only states authority/responsibility for employees.
blocks.ai_reasoning: HP Candidate 0 covers the same core stop-work authority concept: personnel are expected to intervene when work is unsafe. The KCAD text is best treated as a Variant because it is a regional policy statement for Pakistan and it explicitly includes contractors, while the HP chunk is a corporate-wide land-rig statement framed around employees.
blocks.hp_original_text:
## 2.0. Introduction

2.1. Each employee has the authority and responsibility any time they feel an unsafe condition exists, an unsafe work practice is occurring, or improper instructions are given by a co-worker, H&P supervisor, 3rd party Representative, or Operator Representative that may cause harm to people, impact on the environment, or property damage.

blocks.text:
- **Everyone** have the right and duty to refuse to perform any task that they do not completely understand or may feel is unsafe.
- **Everyone** have the authority to stop all work being performed by other employees if they perceive the work is unsafe.
- **No work** will resume until all stop work issues and concerns have been adequately addressed.
- **No one** will be reprimanded for refusal to perform unsafe tasks or utilizing the right and authority to Stop Work.
- **Any** form of retribution or intimidation directed at any individual for exercising their authority as outlined in this Policy will not be tolerated.

No job is so urgent or so important that we cannot take **Time Out For Safety** to continue safely.
blocks.additive_detail: KCAD adds lack of complete understanding as a stop/refusal trigger and states work cannot resume until stop-work concerns are addressed; those details are not shown in HP 2.1.
blocks.ai_reasoning: HP Candidate 0 covers the same core stop-work authority concept: any employee may intervene when work is unsafe. The KCAD text is a regional Pakistan variant that adds a specific trigger of not fully understanding the task, plus broader no-resume and anti-reprisal protections not shown in the retrieved HP chunks.

blocks.text:
# Stop Work Authority Policy Statement

KCA Deutag is committed to achieving safe, effective, trouble-free operations. We firmly believe that an incident and accident-free environment is possible 365 days a year and every incident is preventable.

Health and Safety of employees is one of the Core Values of KCA DEUTAG.
blocks.ai_reasoning: The KCAD text is a policy-level safety commitment statement for Stop Work Authority, while HP candidate 0 provides the operational authority/responsibility statement in the same control domain. That makes candidate 0 complementary rather than equivalent or variant; the other HP chunks are only broadly related because they address document purpose or a resume-work rule rather than the same policy intent.

blocks.text: No job is so urgent or so important that we cannot take **Time Out For Safety** to continue safely.

blocks.hp_original_text:
## 2.0. Introduction

2.1. Each employee has the authority and responsibility any time they feel an unsafe condition exists, an unsafe work practice is occurring, or improper instructions are given by a co-worker, H&P supervisor, 3rd party Representative, or Operator Representative that may cause harm to people, impact on the environment, or property damage.

before.source_heading_path: Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority

after.source_heading_path: Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority

blocks.source_heading_path: Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority

blocks.text: 2.2. Training for Stop Work Authority procedures will be provided to employees at H&P’s New Employee Indoctrination (NEI) prior to reporting for their first job assignment.
2.3. Upon observing a health, safety, or environmental exposure the employee will immediately stop work.
Our vision is that everyone has a responsibility and obligation to stop any work, which is not performed in a safe way.
blocks.source_heading_path: Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority
blocks.ai_reasoning: HP Candidate 1 most directly matches the KCAD statement because both are policy-level role assignments requiring personnel, including subcontractors, to initiate stop work when unsafe work is occurring. The KCAD text does not add a new threshold, role, approval, or trigger beyond what HP already states; it mainly reflects a Kuwait-specific scope, so this is a Tier 2 Variant rather than an Equivalent match.
blocks.hp_original_text: 2.3. Upon observing a health, safety, or environmental exposure the employee will immediately stop work.

blocks.text: 2.4. Employee should approach the person who is performing the unsafe act. If there is an unsafe condition or procedure the employee should notify his supervisor.
2.5. The work will not resume until any unsafe acts, conditions, or procedures have been addressed and resolved.
2.6. Any form of retaliation or intimidation directed at any individual for exercising their authority as outlined in this program will not be tolerated.

- **No one** will be reprimanded for refusal to perform unsafe tasks or utilizing the right and authority to Stop Work.
- **Any** form of retribution or intimidation directed at any individual for exercising their authority as outlined in this Policy will not be tolerated.
blocks.source_heading_path: Policy Statement on Stop Work Authority
blocks.additive_detail: KCAD adds explicit protection against being reprimanded for refusing unsafe tasks or using Stop Work Authority; HP only states that retaliation or intimidation will not be tolerated.
blocks.ai_reasoning: HP Candidate 0 directly overlaps the KCAD anti-retaliation safeguard for exercising Stop Work Authority, but the KCAD text goes further by explicitly barring reprimand for refusing unsafe work. That makes the best match a Tier 1 Variant rather than Equivalent, and there is no visible operational conflict in the provided HP text.
blocks.hp_original_text: 2.6. Any form of retaliation or intimidation directed at any individual for exercising their authority as outlined in this program will not be tolerated.

blocks.text: 2.7. Employees should report their stop work interventions to their supervisor so the intervention may be reviewed for opportunities for improvement.

## 3.0. Roles and Responsibilities

3.1. All Employees and sub-contractors are responsible to initiate a “stop work” intervention when warranted, support the intervention of others, and properly report “stop work” actions.

3.2. Management is responsible for creating a culture where SWA is exercised freely, honor request for “stop work”, work to resolve issues before operations resume, recognized proactive participation and ensure that all “stop work” actions are properly reported with required follow-up completed.

3.3. Supervisors must establish the clear expectation to exercise SWA, create a culture where SWA is exercised freely, follow up with employees once a “stop work” has been addressed, resolve SWA conflicts when they arise and hold those accountable that choose not to comply with established SWA policies.
blocks.heading_path: Stop Work Authority Process > 3.0. Roles and Responsibilities

blocks.text:
## 4.0. Process Steps

4.1. When an employee identifies a perceived unsafe condition, act, error, omission, or lack of understanding that could result in an undesirable event, a “stop work” intervention shall be immediately initiated with the person(s) potentially at risk.
blocks.heading_path: Stop Work Authority Process > 4.0. Process Steps > 4.1

blocks.text: 4.2. “Stop work” interventions should be initiated in a positive manner and should involve open-ended questions to create feedback.
blocks.heading_path: Stop Work Authority Process > 4.0. Process Steps > 4.2

blocks.text: 4.3. Notify all affected personnel and supervision of the “stop work” issue. If necessary, stop associated work activities, remove person(s) from the area, stabilize the situation and make the area as safe as possible.
blocks.heading_path: Stop Work Authority Process > 4.0. Process Steps > 4.3

blocks.text: 4.4. If determined and agreed that the task or operation is safe to proceed as is (i.e., the “stop work” initiator was unaware of certain facts or procedures) the affected persons should give the initiator success feedback for their concern and proceed with the work.
blocks.heading_path: Stop Work Authority Process > 4.0. Process Steps > 4.4

blocks.text: 4.5. If determined and agreed that the stop work issue is valid, then every attempt should be made to resolve the issue to all affected person’s satisfaction prior to the commencement of work.
blocks.heading_path: Stop Work Authority Process > 4.0. Process Steps > 4.5
